/about.title: Echo | SaaS Website Template
/about.description: Echo is a Framer template built for sales intelligence and pipeline tools. Dark UI, scroll animations, pricing tables, testimonials, and a working contact form. Ready to publish.
h1: The system that keeps your pipeline in sync
h2: The people that's running Echo The people that'srunning Echo
h3: Pipeline momentum you can actually see
p: Most sales teams have the data they need. It's just buried across email threads, call recordings, Slack channels, and CRM fields nobody updates. A champion goes quiet, a pricing objection surfaces on a call, a new stakeholder joins late. The signals are there, but no one sees the full picture.

Echo fixes that. It connects to your existing tools and builds a live timeline for every deal. You see what happened, what changed, and where momentum is shifting. Pipeline reviews get shorter because the context is already there. Forecasts get more accurate because they're built on activity, not gut feel.
Every email, call, Slack thread, and meeting tied to a deal — pulled into one timeline. Your reps stop digging through tabs and start selling with the full picture in front of them.
Deals don't stall overnight. Echo tracks how each opportunity moves week over week, so you spot slowdowns early and keep your forecast grounded in what's actually happening.
Revenue teams across SaaS, fintech, and professional services run their pipeline on Echo.
Your deal data is always available. We run on redundant infrastructure with daily backups and SOC 2 compliance.
Email, calendar, Slack, Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, and more. Echo connects to the tools your team already uses.
Over two million deals have been tracked through Echo, that data makes our recommendations sharper every quarter.
Stay up to date with Echo — product updates, pipeline tips, and the occasional thing worth reading. About once a month.

h3: Understand how your pipeline is moving
Clear next steps, right when you need them
h5: Our team is ready to assist you anytime.
h6: How does Echo connect with our existing tools?
What makes Echo different from traditional CRMs?
How does Echo calculate deal health?
Can we customize signals and alerts?
Does Echo replace our existing CRM?
p: Every email, call, and Slack thread tied to one deal view. Your team sells with context, not guesswork.
Stop piecing together emails, notes, and Slack threads. Echo brings every interaction into a single timeline so you can see the full story before you reply, call, or make a decision.
No more staring at a pipeline wondering what to do. Echo looks at activity, momentum, and past outcomes, then suggests the next best move so you can act instead of guess.
Static spreadsheets go stale fast. Echo updates your forecast as deals move, stall, or speed up, giving you a live view of what is likely to close and what needs attention.
Echo logs meetings, captures emails, and summarizes calls in the background. Your CRM stays accurate without the end-of-week data cleanup.
See who is engaged, who has gone quiet, and where risk is building. Echo highlights shifts in response patterns and buying signals before they turn into lost deals.
Type a question in plain language and get instant answers. Find at-risk accounts, stalled deals, or last week’s meetings without digging through tabs.
Instead of a static snapshot, see how deals progress across stages week by week. Echo reveals where momentum builds and where it slows so you can act with context.
4 deals over 50k with strong recent activity.
3 opportunities with no activity in 7 days.
Reply rates down across 3 enterprise deals.
Echo reviews activity, deal momentum, and past outcomes to suggest what to do next. Follow up, escalate, or move forward with context built in.
“We stopped guessing in pipeline reviews. Echo shows us which deals are at risk and what to do next. It changed how we run every week.”
“Echo keeps our CRM accurate without anyone chasing updates. Meetings are summarized, follow-ups suggested, and nothing slips through.”

Echo pulls together every interaction across a deal and shows you what changed, what stalled, and what needs attention next.
For revenue teams that want deal clarity and pipeline visibility without a six-month rollout.
